summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) People & Talent Analytics professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, building insights grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to turn messy people data into clear stories that drive better decisions.

bullets.text: Analyzed {data|2,000+ data points} of employee experience using mixed methods to identify the top driver of engagement
Transformed analysis into {story|data storytelling} that influenced C-suite decisions and continued to be referenced after I left
Built {viz|reports and visualizations} in Excel to surface satisfaction drivers and guide resource allocation
Delivered 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects partnering with People stakeholders on survey design and analysis
As a team of consultants, {data|audited 300+ leave cases} and quantified $362K to refute assumptions with evidence
Influenced policy decisions through {story|data storytelling}, empowering the client team to present executive-ready insights
For a separate NGO client, analyzed {data|qualitative data} from focus groups and interviews to surface decision-making themes
Use {data|enrollment and pipeline data} to monitor program health, surface issues, and inform decisions across 20+ programs
Partner {cross|cross-functionally} with Marketing, Tech, and Sales teams as a data-informed point of contact
Establish {proc|documentation, SOPs, and governance} for 70+ staff, standardizing how information is tracked and shared
Map and improve {proc|processes}, reducing onboarding time by 75% by diagnosing where workflows broke down
Worked in a quantitative trading firm, supporting {rec|recruiting} operations in a fast-paced, data-driven environment
Researched and analyzed global compensation {data|data} to inform a challenging international hiring decision
Tracked {rec|recruiting} pipeline and candidate communication, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
Delivered HR support across {rec|recruiting}, headcount, and succession planning, working from workforce data
Optimized onboarding across 3 global locations by {proc|mapping the process} and clarifying responsibilities
Built a checklist and reporting spreadsheet to track and standardize cross-regional workflows
Strengthened {cross|engagement} via an HR newsletter, recognized among 1,000+ competitors

changeLog: 2026-05-31: Tailored for Discord — Recruiting Data Analyst (SF Bay Area). Broad umbrella 'People & Talent Analytics.' Location → San Francisco, CA. STRETCH — JD requires 4+ yrs analytics + SQL/Snowflake/Tableau/One Model which Jamie does NOT have; tailored honestly around her genuine data-analysis strengths, data storytelling, recruiting/Greenhouse domain, and process/governance. Led with NextGen (2,000+ data points, storytelling that influenced C-suite) then ODN (audit/quant + qual analysis). Swapped Projects to surface her real RAG-based data-system personal project (true, from corpus). Skills: Advanced Excel + reporting/viz + Greenhouse — did NOT claim SQL/Tableau/Snowflake/Python (she lacks them). ODN=3 (both projects, collective), others=4. Cover is candid about the SQL gap.
